parsed_review.summary: CP00-340 is a descriptor-only intake-core review-slice pack covering the 10 RP10.P09.M07 "Test And Golden Case Set" middle rows (S03 Permission bypass questions through S12 Human approval summary). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is internally consistent and accurately scoped: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer="descriptor", status="implemented_pending_review". Identity/scope (risk A, 10 units, range RP10.P09.M07.S03-S12, deliverable split security_audit:2/test:1/ui:1/implementation:6) match across review-baseline, manifest, registry.js binding, intake-core-contract.json projection, the validate script, and closeout-pack-plan.json. The descriptor sets ~40 negative-capability flags (no product-state write, no runtime dispatch, no audit/permission evaluation, no object storage, no Citation Ledger, no Loop, no command runtime, no Hermes runtime receipt, no real/fixture data); these are mirrored in registry/contract no_write_attestations and enforced by validateIntakeCoreCp340Coverage and validateIntakeCoreCp340ReviewSliceDescriptor. Row-level safety guards (permission_bypass_detected=false, leak_detected=false, claude_final_approval_claimed=false, human_final_approval_required=true) bind correctly via inherited CP339 row-extras and are asserted by both the node test suite (model.test.js) and the contract validator, whose deepEqual contract<->descriptor check is satisfied. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only with tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic control-plane evidence labels (receipt_shape descriptor_only ... no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is modeled (forbidden_review_evidence rejects auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, and missing-field outputs) and readiness auth-preflight passed with non-zero stdout. Changed-file scope is clean: 8 active implementation refs; unrelated dirty/untracked files (docx, workbook/*, ldip results) are preserved and not attributed to the pack, with no active untracked implementation files omitted. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Two P3 informational notes (expected pending downstream pipeline artifacts; fail-closed conditional guards verified safe) are recorded and do not block pack or goal closeout. Final enterprise-trust/go-live approval remains a human decision outside this read-only receipt.

findings.title: Post-review pipeline artifacts not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-340/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which are not present yet (Glob shows only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). This is expected: per pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence, these artifacts are produced downstream of this read_only_claude_run step (finding_adjudication, construction_inspection), and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with valid_review_receipts=[]. Their absence is therefore the correct current state and not a missing-evidence defect that should be read as a BLOCK.
findings.recommendation: No action for this review. Ensure the closeout pipeline generates claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json from this verdict before final-closeout validation and commit, so required_pack_artifacts is complete at commit time.

findings.title: Row-level security guards are conditional but verified fail-closed for the security_audit/UI/approval rows
findings.details: validateIntakeCoreCp340ReviewSliceDescriptor guards (permission_bypass_detected, leak_detected, claude_final_approval_claimed, human_final_approval_required) are written as 'if (rows.X && rows.X.field !== false/true)'. The row objects only carry these fields because INTAKE_CORE_CP340_ROW_EXTRAS spreads INTAKE_CORE_CP339_ROW_EXTRAS (service.js:2769-2778), which defines permission_bypass_questions/ui_leak_questions/go_no_go_verdict_format/human_approval_summary. I confirmed the keys produced by intakeCoreRowKey (snake_case) match the literal guard keys, so the guards bind; and if a required field were ever absent the comparison evaluates truthy and pushes an error (fails closed). This was the key check for the S03/S04 security_audit and S06 UI deliverables; behavior is safe.
findings.recommendation: No action required. If future hardening is desired, assert the safety fields unconditionally rather than behind the 'rows.X &&' existence guard to make the intent explicit, but current behavior is fail-closed and correct.
